hero.title: Create Unlimited Digital Product Passports
hero.subtitle: Free, open-source platform for EU Ecodesign for Sustainable Products Regulation (ESPR) compliance. Generate machine-readable passports with QR codes for batteries, textiles, construction products, and 12+ categories. Self-host on your own servers or use our hosted version.

features.title: Everything You Need for Compliance
features.subtitle: Built following EU technical standards: decentralized storage, ISO 15459 identifiers, tiered access rights, and machine-readable JSON/XML output.

espr.title: ESPR Compliant
espr.description: Pre-configured templates following EU Ecodesign for Sustainable Products Regulation requirements per category.

openSource.title: Open Source
openSource.description: Fully open source. Self-host on your own servers for complete data control, or use our hosted version for free.

machineReadable.title: Machine-Readable
machineReadable.description: Not static PDFs. Structured JSON/XML data for integration with BIM, recycling systems, and other software.

qr.title: QR & Data Carriers
qr.description: Each passport gets a unique URL and QR code. Ready for NFC/RFID integration per ISO 15459.

ai.title: Autofill with AI
ai.subtitle: Stop typing manually. Upload a photo of your product label or any document, and our AI extracts all the data automatically in seconds.

snap.title: Snap a Photo
snap.description: Take a picture of your wine label, product packaging, or any label with product information.

upload.title: Upload Documents
upload.description: PDF datasheets, Word documents, Excel specs — upload any file and we'll extract the relevant data.

extract.title: AI Extracts Data
extract.description: Our vision AI reads and understands your documents, filling in all passport fields automatically.

multiUpload.title: Works with multiple uploads
multiUpload.description: Upload multiple photos or documents sequentially — AI merges data intelligently

categories.title: Supported Product Categories
categories.subtitle: Templates built from official EU regulations and delegated acts. Active regulations are in effect, Priority groups have templates ready for upcoming requirements.

timeline.title: EU DPP Implementation Deadlines
timeline.subtitle: The EU is rolling out Digital Product Passport requirements in phases. Start preparing now to ensure compliance.

feb2027.title: February 2027
feb2027.description: EV, industrial, and LMT batteries must have Digital Product Passports with full lifecycle data, carbon footprint declarations (kg CO₂e/kWh with Class A-D rating), and recycled content percentages for cobalt, lithium, and nickel.

active.title: Active Now
active.description: Construction Products (EU 2024/3110), Wine & Spirits (EU 2021/2117), and Toys (EU 2025/2509) already require digital declarations. Templates available now.

rollout.title: 2027-2030: Full Rollout
rollout.description: Textiles, electronics, furniture, iron/steel, aluminum, cosmetics, tires, and detergents will require DPPs as delegated acts are published. Priority group templates ready now.

selfHost.title: Run It On Your Own Servers
selfHost.subtitle: This project is fully open source. You can self-host on your own infrastructure for complete data sovereignty, modify the code to fit your needs, or contribute to the project.

deploy.title: Self-Host
deploy.description: Deploy on your own servers. Full control over your data and compliance documentation.

customize.title: Customize
customize.description: Add custom fields, integrate with your ERP/PLM systems, or build white-label solutions.

contribute.title: Contribute
contribute.description: Help improve templates as regulations evolve. Community-driven compliance updates.

cta.title: Ready to Get Compliant?
cta.subtitle: Start creating EU-compliant Digital Product Passports today. Free, unlimited, and open source.

auth.subtitle: Sign in or create an account

passport.dppNameHelp: This name is only visible on your dashboard
passport.productNamePlaceholder: Enter a name for this product
passport.productNameHelp: This is the name consumers will see on the public Digital Product Passport
passport.unsavedChangesDesc: You have unsaved changes. Are you sure you want to leave? Your changes will be lost.
passport.notFoundDesc: This digital product passport doesn't exist or has been removed.
passport.noAdditionalInfo: No additional information required for this category.

imageUpload.loginRequired: You must be logged in to upload images

categoryDescriptions.battery: Carbon footprint, recycling, supply chain due diligence
categoryDescriptions.electronics: Repairability index, spare parts, software updates
categoryDescriptions.iron_steel: Carbon intensity, scrap content, alloy chemistry
categoryDescriptions.furniture: EUDR wood origin, spare parts, PFAS
categoryDescriptions.other: Generic DPP template for any product type

wine.smallQuantitiesDesc: Values of 0 will be displayed as "Contains small quantities of..."
wine.additionalNutritionalDesc: These values will be included in the nutritional declaration
wine.electronicLabelOptionsDesc: Choose which analysis data to show on the electronic label (optional)
wine.displaySettingsDesc: Configure what appears on your public Digital Product Passport
wine.hidePromo: Hide "Powered by Digital - Product - Passports .com" promotional message
wine.ingredientsDescription: Select ingredients from the wine ingredient list or add custom ones. Sort them by quantity (largest to smallest).
wine.chooseIngredientsDescription: Select all ingredients that are part of your wine composition. If necessary, you can add a custom ingredient.
wine.sortIngredientsDescription: The ingredient list should be sorted from largest to smallest quantity. Quantities below 0.1 g/100 ml don't need to be sorted.
wine.allergenWarning: This wine contains allergens that must be declared:
wine.recyclingDescription: Select the materials used for your wine packaging.
wine.recyclingNote: Check your local recycling guidelines. Separate components and dispose of them correctly.
wine.negligibleAmounts: Contains negligible amounts of fat, saturates, protein and salt.

hints.productName: The commercial name of your wine as it appears on the label.
hints.productNameTooltip: This is the official product name that consumers see. It should match exactly what's printed on your wine label, including any vintage year or special designations.
hints.grapeVariety: The type(s) of grape used to make this wine.
hints.grapeVarietyTooltip: List the grape varieties (e.g., Cabernet Sauvignon, Merlot, Chardonnay). If it's a blend, you can list multiple varieties. This helps consumers understand the wine's character and is required by EU labeling rules when variety is mentioned on the label.
hints.vintage: The year the grapes were harvested.
hints.vintageTooltip: The vintage year indicates when the grapes were harvested. Use 'NV' (Non-Vintage) for wines blended from multiple years, common in Champagne. Multi-vintage wines blend grapes from different harvest years.
hints.volume: The amount of wine in the bottle.
hints.volumeTooltip: Standard wine bottles are 750ml. Other common sizes: 375ml (half bottle), 1.5L (Magnum), 3L (Double Magnum). This must match the volume printed on your label.
hints.countryTooltip: The country where the grapes were grown and the wine was made. This is a legal requirement for wine sold in the EU and affects which regulations apply to your product.
hints.regionTooltip: The geographic region within the country where the wine was produced (e.g., Bordeaux, Tuscany, Napa Valley). This can indicate quality and style characteristics protected by geographical indication laws.
hints.denomination: Official quality classification or protected origin.
hints.denominationTooltip: Protected designations like AOC (Appellation d'Origine Contrôlée), AOP (Appellation d'Origine Protégée), DOC, DOCG, or IGP (Indication Géographique Protégée). These EU-protected terms guarantee the wine meets specific quality and origin requirements.
hints.sugarClassification: The sweetness level of the wine.
hints.sugarClassificationTooltip: For sparkling wines: Brut Nature (0-3g/L), Extra Brut (0-6g/L), Brut (0-12g/L), Extra Dry (12-17g/L), Dry/Sec (17-32g/L), Demi-Sec (32-50g/L), Doux (50+g/L). For still wines, terms like 'dry', 'medium', 'sweet' are used.
hints.producerName: The winery or company that made the wine.
hints.producerNameTooltip: The legal name of the producer, estate, or winery. This is required on wine labels and helps consumers identify who is responsible for the product.
hints.bottlerInfo: Who bottled the wine and where.
hints.bottlerInfoTooltip: EU law requires stating who bottled the wine and their address. Use phrases like 'Bottled by [Name], [Address]' or 'Mis en bouteille au château' (estate bottled). This may differ from the producer if a négociant bottles the wine.
hints.alcoholPercentTooltip: The percentage of alcohol in the wine, measured by volume. EU law requires this on the label with ±0.5% tolerance. Typical wines range from 5.5% (light sparkling) to 15%+ (fortified wines). This value is used to calculate energy content.
hints.residualSugarTooltip: The amount of natural grape sugar (in grams per liter) that wasn't converted to alcohol during fermentation. This affects sweetness perception and is used to calculate carbohydrate content. Dry wines typically have less than 4g/L.
hints.totalAcidity: The total acid content expressed as tartaric acid.
hints.totalAcidityTooltip: Total acidity is measured in grams per liter as tartaric acid equivalent (C₄H₆O₆). This affects the wine's taste, freshness, and stability. Typical values range from 4-9 g/L. This is used in the nutritional calculation.
hints.glycerineTooltip: Glycerine (glycerol) is a natural byproduct of alcoholic fermentation that contributes to wine's body and mouthfeel. Typical values range from 4-15 g/L. Higher alcohol wines tend to have more glycerine. Used in energy calculations.
hints.calculatedValuesTooltip: These nutritional values are automatically calculated from alcohol, sugar, acidity, and glycerine using EU-standard formulas. Check 'Manual' to enter your own laboratory-tested values instead.
hints.energyTooltip: Energy content calculated from alcohol (7 kcal/g), sugar (4 kcal/g), and glycerine (2.4 kcal/g). This is what appears on the nutrition label.
hints.carbohydratesTooltip: Total carbohydrates are calculated as the sum of residual sugar and tartaric acid content per 100ml. This includes all sugars and acids that contribute to carbohydrate content.
hints.sugarNutritionTooltip: The sugar value shown on nutrition labels, calculated from residual sugar content per 100ml. Different from residual sugar (g/L) – this is per 100ml as required by EU food labeling.
hints.smallQuantitiesHint: Wine naturally contains very small amounts of these nutrients. Leave at 0 unless lab analysis shows otherwise.
hints.smallQuantitiesTooltip: EU Regulation 1169/2011 allows stating 'Contains negligible amounts' for nutrients present in very small quantities. For wine, fat, saturated fat, protein, and salt are typically negligible. Only enter values here if laboratory analysis shows measurable amounts.
hints.electronicLabelHint: Optional data to display alongside the required nutrition information.
hints.electronicLabelTooltip: Beyond the mandatory nutrition declaration, you can optionally show additional analysis data like actual alcohol %, residual sugar, or acidity. This provides transparency for consumers and sommeliers who want detailed wine information.
hints.displaySettingsHint: Control additional elements shown on your public passport page.

counterfeit.title: Counterfeit Protection
counterfeit.description: Protect your product against counterfeiting with a security seal from our partner.
counterfeit.enabledDescription: An email has been sent to our counterfeit protection partner. They will contact you to deliver the security seal.
counterfeit.requestSentDescription: An email has been sent to our counterfeit protection partner. They will contact you to deliver the security seal.
counterfeit.errorNoEmail: Unable to send request - user email not found
counterfeit.errorSaveFirst: Please save the passport first before enabling counterfeit protection
counterfeit.errorFailed: Failed to send counterfeit protection request

translation.title: Translations
translation.description: Provide translations for all EU languages. Use AI to generate initial translations, then review and edit as needed.
translation.enterTextFirst: Please enter text in the field before generating translations.
translation.reviewAndEdit: Review and edit translations as needed.
translation.errorDesc: Failed to generate translations. Please try again.

ai.supportedFormats: Supports photos, PDFs, and Word documents
ai.multiUploadTip: Have multiple files? Run this again after each upload to merge data from different sources.
ai.scannerDescription: Upload a wine label photo, PDF, or document and AI will extract the information automatically. You can also scan a photo of a QR code from an existing Digital Product Passport.
ai.experimentalWarning: Experimental feature: Please double-check all extracted data before saving. AI may make mistakes.
ai.autofillCompleteDesc: Fields have been populated. Please review and verify the data before saving.
ai.qrCodeUsed: A QR code was detected and its linked page was also analyzed for additional data.
ai.noDataExtracted: No data extracted from the label
ai.processingFailedDesc: Failed to extract data from the label
ai.quotaExceededDesc: You've reached the monthly limit of 100 AI label scans.
ai.quotaExceededBody: To ensure fair access for all users, we limit AI-powered label scanning to 100 scans per month. Your quota will reset at the beginning of next month.
ai.quotaContactBody: If you need a higher limit for your business, please contact us and we'll be happy to discuss options.
